name: CLI
description: Build the cobra CLI with generate, init, and watch commands, plus the Writer package for doc comment injection. The Writer handles existing-comment detection (//, ///, #, """, /** styles), force-overwrite mode with comment block replacement, CRLF/LF preservation, build directive preservation, and dry-run/diff-only preview modes. The CLI exposes --diff/-d, --dry-run, --force/-f, and --config/-c flags, plus deoxy init for .deoxy.yaml scaffolding and a skeleton deoxy watch for fsnotify-based file watching.
anti_goals: Do NOT build the VS Code extension sidecar (Phase 4)
Do NOT implement smart text / name inference (Phase 5 — getter/setter/ctor detection, name splitting)
Do NOT add AI-generated descriptions (out of scope permanently)
Do NOT implement full watch-mode regeneration (skeleton only — prints events, no regeneration callback)
Do NOT add stdin/stdout JSON-RPC mode for IDE integration (Phase 4)
Do NOT add alignment formatting for @param tags (Phase 5)
roadmap_success_criteria: deoxy generate ./src finds all supported source files, generates doc comments, and writes them back in-place
deoxy generate --diff ./src shows proposed changes without modifying files
deoxy generate --dry-run ./src processes files but does not write any output
deoxy generate --force overwrites existing doc comments with regenerated ones
deoxy init creates a .deoxy.yaml config file with sensible defaults
deoxy watch ./src prints fsnotify events (skeleton; no regeneration callback yet)
Generated Go files parse correctly after comment insertion (no syntax errors)
Existing comments are detected and respected (skip mode by default; --force overwrites)
Build directives (//go:build, //go:generate) are not broken by comment insertion
Original line endings (LF/CRLF) are preserved in output

tasks.title: Create internal/writer/writer.go — Writer struct with comment injection logic
tasks.description: Build the Writer package that takes generated doc comments from the generator pipeline and injects them into source files. Core capabilities: detect existing comments above a symbol line (hasExistingComment), find the full extent of an existing comment block for force-overwrite (findCommentBlock), insert new comment text with proper indentation (insertComment), split/join source lines with LF/CRLF preservation (toLines/fromLines), and orchestrate the full apply-modify-write cycle (applyComments). Writer supports three modes: normal (write to disk), dry-run (compute edits but don't write), and diff-only (compute modified content for display but don't write).

FileEdit.description: A set of insertions to make in a single file.

fields.name: Original
fields.comment: Original file content for diff calculation

fields.name: Modified
fields.comment: Content after all insertions (nil if no changes)

Insertion.description: A single doc comment to insert before a symbol.

Writer.description: Manages the process of injecting doc comments into source files.
methods: func New(gen *generator.Generator, force, diffOnly, dryRun bool) *Writer
func (w *Writer) Generate(filePath string) ([]FileEdit, error) — process single file
func (w *Writer) GenerateDir(dirPath string) ([]FileEdit, error) — process directory
func (w *Writer) GenerateAll(scanResult *scanner.Result) ([]FileEdit, error) — process all from scanner

hasExistingComment.behavior: Scans upward from lineIdx-1, skipping blank lines. Returns true if a comment line is found (detects //, ///, #, """, ''', /*, *, */ prefixes).

findCommentBlock.behavior: Finds the full contiguous comment block above the given line. Returns (startLine, endLine) inclusive-exclusive range. Only called when force=true to determine what to remove before inserting the new comment.

insertComment.signature: insertComment(lines []string, lineIdx int, comment string, indent string) []string
insertComment.behavior: Inserts a doc comment before lineIdx with the given indentation. Adds a leading blank line if the line above the insertion point is not empty. Returns new slice with comment inserted.

toLines.behavior: Splits source bytes into lines, detecting LF vs CRLF separator, and noting whether the source ended with a trailing newline.

fromLines.behavior: Joins lines back with the detected separator, restoring trailing newline if original had one.

extractIndent.behavior: Returns the leading whitespace (spaces/tabs) of the given line.

insertion_ordering.description: applyComments sorts insertions by StartLine descending (bottom-up) so that earlier insertions don't change line offsets for later ones. When force=true and an existing comment block is found, the block is removed first, then the new comment is inserted. The StartLine for subsequent insertions is adjusted by the number of lines removed.

error_handling.file_not_found: Generate returns error if file path does not exist
error_handling.parse_error: Generate returns error with file path context if generator.Parse fails
error_handling.write_failure: applyComments returns error if os.WriteFile fails
error_handling.directory_scan: GenerateDir wraps scanner.Scan errors with context

specification.rationale: Writer is the final stage of the deoxy pipeline. Bottom-up insertion (sorting descending by StartLine) is essential to maintain correct line offsets as insertions shift subsequent lines. The toLines/fromLines roundtrip preserves exact line endings and trailing newlines for minimal diff noise. hasExistingComment and findCommentBlock are split into two functions for clarity: the first is a boolean check for the skip-existing default, the second computes the exact range for force-overwrite removal.

success_criteria: Writer accepts a generator.Generator and produces FileEdit results
hasExistingComment detects Go (//), Rust (///), Python (#), Python docstrings ("""/'''), and block comments (/**/)
hasExistingComment returns false when no comment exists above the target line
findCommentBlock returns correct inclusive-exclusive range for contiguous comment blocks
findCommentBlock returns (-1,-1) when no comment exists (non-force path doesn't call it)
insertComment inserts comment at correct position with proper indentation
insertComment adds leading blank line when preceding line is non-empty
toLines/fromLines roundtrip preserves LF and CRLF line endings
toLines/fromLines preserves trailing newline status
Build directives (//go:build) before functions are preserved after insertion
go vet ./internal/writer/ passes with zero warnings

tasks.title: Create internal/writer/writer_test.go — Writer test suite
tasks.description: Write comprehensive tests for the Writer package covering all core functions and edge cases. Tests use table-driven patterns, parallel subtests, and real fixture files for integration scenarios.

subtests.name: leading-blank
subtests.desc: Leading blank line added when line above non-empty

subtests.name: leading-blank-indented
subtests.desc: Leading blank line with indented target

subtests.name: with-blank-lines
subtests.desc: Blank lines between comment and symbol

subtests.name: lineidx-zero
subtests.desc: Function at line 0 has no comment

subtests.name: mixed
subtests.desc: Line with mixed spaces and tabs

subtests.name: lf-no-trailing
subtests.desc: LF line endings, no trailing newline

test_cases.name: TestIntegrationInsertAndDetect
test_cases.description: Full integration: insert comment into Go source, then verify hasExistingComment detects it
assertions: Inserted comment is detected by hasExistingComment

test_cases.name: TestBuildDirectivesPreserved
test_cases.description: Build directives (//go:build, //go:generate) before the function are preserved after comment insertion
assertions: Build directive lines remain before the function
The order is: build directive, blank line, doc comment, blank line, function
No build directive lines are consumed by comment insertion

test_cases.name: TestCRLFPreserved
test_cases.description: CRLF line endings are preserved through the full write cycle
assertions: Comment lines use CRLF to match source

specification.test_data: Tests use inline test data constructed in test functions. Integration tests create temp files in t.TempDir() for isolation.

success_criteria: All 9 test functions with 29+ subtests pass
TestInsertComment covers: simple, indented, multi-line, leading blank line scenarios
TestHasExistingComment covers all 5 comment styles plus blank line and edge cases
TestToLinesAndFromLines covers LF/CRLF with/without trailing newline
Integration test verifies detect-after-insert roundtrip
Build directive preservation test verifies //go:build, //go:generate before function
CRLF preservation test verifies roundtrip through full write cycle

tasks.title: Create cmd/deoxy/cmd/ — Cobra root command with generate, init, watch subcommands
tasks.description: Build the cobra CLI with root command (deoxy), generate (deoxy generate [path...]), init (deoxy init [dir]), and watch (deoxy watch [path...]) subcommands. The root command shows project description and version. Generate accepts --diff/-d, --dry-run, --force/-f, --config/-c flags. Init scaffolds .deoxy.yaml. Watch is a skeleton that uses fsnotify to print file events. All commands use RunE returning nil for silent-completion (no cobra default help output). Output uses cmd.Printf/cmd.Println for proper test capture through cobra's output chain.

root.long: deoxy generates doc comments for multiple programming languages using tree-sitter AST analysis.
root.behavior: Prints help by default (RunE delegates to help). RunE returns nil for silent completion.

generate.short: Generate doc comments for source files

flags.name: diff
flags.usage: Show diff of changes without writing

flags.name: dry-run
flags.usage: Process files but do not write

generate.behavior: For each path: if directory, recursively discover source files; else process single file. Runs generator pipeline (config → scan → parse → render), then writer for injection. Reports summary with file count, symbol count, error count.

init.behavior: Creates .deoxy.yaml in the specified directory (or current directory) with default config. Errors if file already exists.

watch.short: [experimental] Watch files for changes and regenerate docs
watch.behavior: Skeleton implementation: uses fsnotify to watch paths, prints created/written/removed events. No regeneration callback yet.

file_updates.main.go: Updated to call cmd.Execute() instead of printing version string.
file_updates.main_test.go: Updated to test cobra-based entry point.

specification.output_pattern: All commands use cmd.Printf/cmd.Println instead of fmt.Printf so output routes through cobra's output chain for test capture via rootCmd.SetOut(buf).

success_criteria: deoxy --help shows root help with subcommand listing
deoxy generate --help shows all 4 flags (--diff, --dry-run, --force, --config)
deoxy init --help shows usage with optional directory argument
deoxy init creates .deoxy.yaml with default config
deoxy init fails with error if .deoxy.yaml already exists
deoxy init fails with error for non-existent directory
deoxy generate processes files and produces output
All cobra commands use RunE returning nil (no default help output)
go vet ./cmd/deoxy/... passes with zero warnings

tasks.title: Update go.mod/go.sum — Add cobra and fsnotify dependencies
tasks.description: Add github.com/spf13/cobra and github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify as direct dependencies. Run go mod tidy to clean up go.sum. Verify no dependency conflicts.

specification.rationale: Cobra is the de facto standard Go CLI framework. Fsnotify is the standard cross-platform file watcher (used by air, reflex, and other Go dev tools). Both are mature, well-maintained libraries with zero transitive dependency conflicts.

success_criteria: go.mod contains cobra and fsnotify as direct dependencies
go.sum is populated after go mod tidy
go build ./... succeeds with all dependencies resolved

tasks.title: Create cmd/deoxy/cmd/generate_test.go — CLI command tests
tasks.description: Write tests for the cobra CLI commands. Uses cobra's direct RunE invocation pattern to avoid global flag state pollution across test functions. Covers: help output for all commands, init functional tests (creates config, fails if exists, fails for non-existent dir), and generate integration tests (dry-run preserves files, diff shows changes without writing, force overwrites existing comments, skip existing by default).

test_cases.name: TestInitCreatesConfig
assertions: File is created at correct path

test_cases.name: TestInitFailsWithNonExistentDir
assertions: Error message mentions directory or path

test_cases.name: TestGenerateWithNonexistentPath
test_cases.description: Generate returns error for non-existent file path

test_cases.name: TestGenerateDryRun
test_cases.description: Generate --dry-run processes but does not write
assertions: Output contains warning that file was skipped or not written
Source file content is unchanged after run

test_cases.name: TestGenerateDiff
assertions: Source file content is unchanged after run

test_cases.name: TestGenerateForce
assertions: Old comment text is no longer in file

test_cases.name: TestGenerateSkipsExistingByDefault
test_cases.description: Generate without --force skips symbols with existing comments
assertions: Function with existing comment is not modified

specification.test_pattern: Direct RunE invocation: set global flag variables, call generateCmd.RunE(cmd, args), capture output via rootCmd.SetOut(buf). Ensures no cobra global state pollution across test functions.

success_criteria: All 11 test functions pass with go test ./cmd/deoxy/cmd/ -v -count=1
Help output tests verify all 4 commands produce expected content
Init tests cover: create, fail-if-exists, fail-on-nonexistent-dir
Generate tests cover: nonexistent path, dry-run, diff, force, skip-existing
Tests use direct RunE pattern (not rootCmd.Execute())
No flag state pollution across test functions

tasks.title: Writer force-overwrite mode and cmd output routing fixes
tasks.description: Implement missing force-overwrite mode in Writer (findCommentBlock, applyComments for removal before insertion) and fix cmd output routing by switching from fmt.Printf to cmd.Printf for proper test capture. Also fix indented comment test expectation for leading blank line behavior.

fixes.fix: Add findCommentBlock to detect contiguous comment block extent; modify applyComments to remove old block before inserting new comment

fixes.issue: fmt.Printf output not captured in tests
fixes.fix: Replace fmt.Printf/fmt.Println with cmd.Printf/cmd.Println in generate.go

fixes.issue: Indented comment test expected wrong output
fixes.fix: Update test expectation to include leading blank line (insertComment always adds one when adjacent to non-empty code)

success_criteria: TestGenerateForce passes: --force overwrites existing comments
TestGenerateDiff captures output via cmd buffer instead of stdout
All writer tests pass with corrected expectations
go vet passes with zero warnings

execution_order.rationale: Writer package (P3-T1) is the core of Phase 3 with no Go dependency on other Phase 3 tasks. Dependency update (P3-T4) is independent — go get cobra/fsnotify doesn't depend on any code changes.
Writer tests (P3-T2) depend on writer.go. Cobra CLI commands (P3-T3) depend on the Writer package and are new files in cmd/deoxy/cmd/. Both can be built in parallel.

Cmd tests (P3-T5) depend on CLI commands (P3-T3) being complete. Force-overwrite and cmd output routing fixes (P3-T6) depend on understanding what's missing from both Writer and CLI — these are post-implementation fixes discovered during testing.
